title: Kosteletzkya mexicana
taxonomy: Kosteletzkya mexicana is a member of the family Malvaceae. It is commonly known as the Semivestite or Mexican thistle flower. This plant belongs to the genus Kosteletzkya, which consists of about 20 species of flowering plants. Kosteletzkya mexicana is native to Mexico and Central America, and is often found growing in wetlands or near water sources. It is classified as a perennial herb, with a growth habit that ranges from 1-2 meters in height.

The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of wounds, fever, and gastrointestinal issues.
